title: transaction — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / erase). This module registers five hooks: tenant provisioning (records the opening balance), tenant erasure archiving (archives to compliance-storage BEFORE the FK CASCADE), tenant erasure purge (the FK CASCADE), a defensive observer hook (rejects negative amounts), and a snapshotting hook (freezes exchange rate on cross-currency transactions).

hooks.name: RecordOpeningBalanceOnTenantProvisioned
hooks.purpose: Records the tenant's opening balance transaction. Creates one transaction (kind='opening_balance', reference_type='TenantProvisioning', reference_id=tenant.id) with ledger entries at zero for every enabled account. When migrating from a legacy system, the migration flow can inject non-zero opening balances via the TransactionRecorder's opening_balance API — the hook then no-ops for that tenant. Fires exactly once per tenant + emits TenantOpeningBalanceRecorded.
hooks.compliance: SOX 404 — every tenant's ledger has a documented origin point (the opening balance transaction). Regulators expect to see the ledger start-of-history entry.

hooks.name: PurgeTransactionDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK CASCADE across transactions + transaction_ledger_entries. IMPORTANT: this hook runs AFTER ArchiveTransactionsToComplianceOnTenantErased has completed successfully. If archive fails, this hook refuses to cascade (returns TRANSACTION_ARCHIVE_INCOMPLETE) — the tenant's transactional data remains in the operational store until an ops-authored fix lands. The hook writes a compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity (transactions + transaction_ledger_entries purged).
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 §3 — right-to-erasure carve-out for financial records. The tenant's operational data is deleted; the compliance archive lives on.

hooks.name: ArchiveTransactionsToComplianceOnTenantErased
hooks.purpose: Runs BEFORE PurgeTransactionDataForErasedTenant. Exports every transaction + its ledger entries + the base_currency-normalized global tenant balance snapshot to compliance-storage as JSONL keyed by (tenant_id, effective_date). The archive lives at least the tenant's lifetime beyond deletion + is retrievable during regulator audits years later. If the archive fails, this hook throws + the FK CASCADE never runs — the tenant's data is preserved in the operational store until ops recovers.
hooks.compliance: SOX 802 + AML — financial records survive tenant erasure. The compliance/archive module owns the storage backend (S3 with Object Lock + Glacier tiering for cold storage).
hooks.priority: runs BEFORE PurgeTransactionDataForErasedTenant — enforced by hook priority ordering on the tenancy::TenantErased subscribe chain

hooks.name: PreventNegativeAmountsOnObserver
hooks.listens_to: internal — invoked by TransactionObserver.creating during pre-persist validation
hooks.purpose: Defense-in-depth guard. Rejects transaction insertions where amount_cents <= 0 (should already be caught by the valid_positive_amount rule but this hook is a belt-and-suspenders check specifically to protect against a bypassed validator). Direction is expressed via ledger entries — never via a negative amount. Refusal returns TRANSACTION_NEGATIVE_AMOUNT + fires a critical audit signal.
hooks.compliance: GAAP — direction is expressed via debit/credit, never by amount sign.

hooks.name: FreezeExchangeRateOnCrossCurrencyTransaction
hooks.listens_to: internal — invoked by TransactionObserver.creating when currency != tenant.base_currency
hooks.purpose: Ensures exchange_rate_to_base + base_amount_cents are captured atomically inside the same DB transaction as the transaction row insert. Delegates to ExchangeRateResolver to fetch the current rate (with 1h cache TTL) + validates the caller-supplied base_amount_cents matches the computed value within ±1 cent rounding tolerance. The rate is IMMUTABLE after commit — historical reports use the frozen rate, never the current market rate. Fires TransactionCurrencyConverted (afterCommit).
hooks.compliance: IAS 21 (IFRS) + IFRS foreign-exchange-rates standard — the transaction-date rate is frozen at commit.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Transaction does not need a per-request tenant hydration path. The tenant context is resolved via tenant.resolve middleware; every recorder + reader gets the tenant from that context. There is no per-request state to hydrate.
